topic: If I had to make my first $100 with AI in 7 days, I'd do this
tweets:
If I had to make my first $100 with AI in the next 7 days, I would NOT start a SaaS.

I'd sell a simple service.

Here's exactly what I'd do:

1. Pick one boring offer:

- Twitter post packages
- Simple blog writing
- Resume rewrites

Not fancy.
Sellable.

2. Use ChatGPT or Claude to make 3 strong samples.

No client yet? Doesn't matter.
Create mock samples for a local business, a coach, or a creator.

3. Put the samples in a Google Doc or simple PDF.

You do not need a website.
You need proof you can solve one problem.

4. DM 20 small businesses or creators.

Short message:
'I made 3 sample posts for your brand. Want me to send them?'

Most people overcomplicate outreach.

5. Deliver with AI.

Use:
- ChatGPT for drafts
- Canva for polish
- Grammarly for cleanup

Ship fast. Get the first payment. Then improve.

Your first $100 with AI probably won't come from a crazy idea.

It'll come from a boring offer, sent to the right person, at the right time.

Would you rather sell content, resumes, or design first?

topic: Most AI side hustles are a waste of time
tweets:
Most AI side hustles are a waste of time.

Not because AI is bad.
Because beginners keep collecting tools instead of building offers.

What people do:

- Try 12 tools in 5 days
- Watch 40 YouTube videos
- Make $0

Research feels productive. It usually isn't.

What actually sells is boring:

- Content packages
- Simple design work
- Resume rewrites
- Lead generation
- Basic automation setup

Nobody brags about boring. Boring still gets paid.

You do not need 20 tools.

Honestly, 3 is enough:
- ChatGPT or Claude
- Canva
- One delivery tool

That's enough to start selling something this week.

The best AI business model in the beginning is not software.

It's service.

Because service teaches you what people will actually pay for.

After you get paid 3-5 times, THEN automate.

That is the part people skip.

They want passive income before active proof.

The boring methods usually make the most money.

Pick one offer.
Use one tool stack.
Sell one result.
Repeat for 30 days.

Which offer would you test first?

topic: A one-person AI income system using Twitter, GitHub, and one affiliate link
tweets:
A one-person AI income system does not need a team.

It needs 3 things:
- attention
- an asset
- one clear offer

Here's the setup:

1. Use Twitter for attention.

Post daily:
- short tips
- tool breakdowns
- hot takes
- simple income ideas

Not everything needs to go viral. It needs to compound.

2. Use GitHub as the asset.

Why?
Because a repo feels useful, searchable, and shareable.

One good tools list can bring traffic for months.

3. Put a free download inside the system.

Example:
- AI tools list
- prompt pack
- starter guide

People rarely follow for 'content'.
They follow for utility.

4. Add one affiliate link that actually fits the audience.

Not 10 links.
One.

If your audience wants to build online income, a tool like Systeme.io makes sense.

5. Turn the loop into a system:

Twitter post -> GitHub visit -> free resource -> affiliate click

Simple loop. Small wins. Real compounding.

Most people try to monetize too late or too early.

Too late = no offer.
Too early = no trust.

The sweet spot is simple value + one relevant next step.

Would you build this as a solo system?
